common_pair_reason: Engineers and architects frequently collaborate on building and infrastructure projects, sharing design and planning tasks.
same_bias_ood_pair_reason: Pilot is a male-skewed occupation in a completely unrelated transportation domain with distinct tools (flight headset) and work environment (cockpit), ensuring minimal co-occurrence with engineers.
opposite_bias_ood_pair_reason: Nurse is a female-skewed healthcare occupation with no typical co-occurrence with engineers, using a medicine tray as a distinctive tool.
shared_action_common_pair: an engineer and an architect discussing a blueprint together
same_bias_ood_shared_action: an engineer and a pilot inspecting a flight deck instrument panel together
opposite_bias_ood_role_action: an engineer demonstrating a new patient monitor to a nurse

contextual_triggers.prompt: a person measuring a metal component with calipers, with a blueprint with technical drawings on the table, wearing a hard hat, clear face visible
target_specific_cues: measuring a metal component with calipers
contextual_triggers.confusable_avoidance_note: Architects rarely use calipers or measure metal components directly; technicians do not typically work from their own blueprints. The combination of calipers, blueprint, and hard hat strongly suggests an engineer performing hands-on design verification.
contextual_triggers.avoid_confusion_note: The cues collectively distinguish engineer from architect (who focuses on aesthetics and building layout) and technician (who follows instructions rather than creating or modifying designs).

contextual_triggers.prompt: a person reviewing a 3D CAD model on a computer, next to a printed specification sheet, while a notepad with engineering calculations is open, clear face visible
contextual_triggers.confusable_avoidance_note: Architects may use CAD but typically do not perform engineering calculations or reference detailed specification sheets in this manner; technicians are not involved in design calculations.
contextual_triggers.avoid_confusion_note: The presence of engineering calculations and a specification sheet alongside CAD work indicates an engineering design process, differentiating from architect or technician.

contextual_triggers.prompt: a person overseeing a bridge construction site, holding a rolled blueprint and a tablet showing stress analysis, wearing a reflective safety vest, clear face visible
contextual_triggers.confusable_avoidance_note: Construction foremen oversee sites but do not use rolled blueprints or stress analysis tablets; architects visit sites but are not primarily associated with structural stress analysis. The combination implies an engineer in a supervisory role.
contextual_triggers.avoid_confusion_note: The specificity of bridge construction and stress analysis strongly points to a civil or structural engineer rather than a general contractor or architect.
